place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast area and wanted to share my thoughts on East Coast Park. It remains one of the best-maintained spaces in Singapore, and its extensive connectivity to other parks makes it an ideal destination for long walks or cycling trips. I particularly enjoyed the ability to ride my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay via the park connector; it was always such a calm and refreshing experience.

The park offers a wonderful atmosphere with its blend of lush greenery and sea views. It is exceptionally well-equipped for families and fitness enthusiasts, featuring dedicated exercise stations, children’s play areas, and wide, separate paths for cycling and walking that prevent the space from feeling crowded.

Beyond the scenery, the facilities are excellent. There are convenient bicycle rentals, a variety of dining options in the restaurant area where you can enjoy the sea breeze, and well-maintained BBQ pits and shelters. For those interested in staying longer, the designated camping areas are a great touch, and I’ve always found the washrooms and bathing facilities to be very clean.

Overall, it is a perfect place to relax, exercise, and enjoy nature, whether you are visiting alone or with friends and family.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast Park is one of the best and most well-maintained parks in Singapore. It is a very big park and is beautifully connected to other parks across Singapore, making it perfect for long walks or cycling trips. The park has lots of nice scenery, with greenery and sea views that make the atmosphere very relaxing. In some areas, there are exercise equipment stations and children’s play areas, which are great for families and fitness lovers.

There is a dedicated cycling path and a separate walking path, both of which are wide and well maintained, so it never feels crowded. You can also rent bicycles, and there are fun activities and attractions that bring in many people. The park has a restaurant area where you can enjoy a variety of foods while feeling the cool sea breeze. You can book BBQ pits, and there are huts available for shelter in case of rain. There are also designated camping areas for those who want to stay overnight.

I used to live near the East Coast area, and what I loved most was riding my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay, as the park connector makes this possible. It was always a calm and refreshing experience. On top of that, the washrooms and bathing areas are very clean and well maintained. Overall, East Coast Park is a perfect place to relax, exercise, eat, and enjoy nature, whether alone, with friends, or with family.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: This is a large swimming complex and great for kids on the 5th floor. There are 3 pools. One for small kiddies, one intermediate and one advanced. Pool is not heated but good for warm weather days. The Olympic pool has a shelter. There is an activesg gym on the 6th floor that overlooks the pool if you want to work out (separate entrance fee of 2.50 sgd). However, the gym are strict with footwear and need to bring your own towel.

review_text: So many activities here. U got library, gym, multi purpose hall, swimming pool, classes for booking. Amazing place

review_text:
Our Tampines Hub (OTH) continues to be one of the most vibrant community spaces in Singapore. Even on a late weekday evening, the energy here is unmistakable. Here’s a review from my recent 8:30 PM visit.

🏮 Festive Vibes: Chinese New Year 2026

The Hub is currently decked out for Chinese New Year, and the atmosphere is fantastic.

• CNY Activities: There are plenty of festive displays and activities scattered throughout the ground floor, from zodiac-themed decorations to cultural showcases. It adds a wonderful touch of tradition to the modern architecture.

• The Crowd: Even at 8:30 PM, the place was quite crowded. It clearly remains the "heart" of Tampines, with families and residents soaking in the festive spirit late into the evening.

🎬 Entertainment: The Arena & Festive Plaza

One of the best features of OTH is the dedicated space for public viewing.

• Live Performances & Movies: The Festive Plaza (the large open atrium) is essentially an arena for the community. There’s almost always something on the "Big Ass Screen"—whether it's a live performance, a football match, or a free movie screening. It’s a great spot to just sit on the steps and unwind.

• The "Big Ass Fans": Despite being an open-air
concept, the cooling is impressive. The massive HVLS (High Volume Low Speed) fans—or "big ass fans" as many call them—keep the air circulating beautifully. Even in our humid weather, the breeze makes the space comfortable for hanging out.

🍛 Food & Convenience

If you're feeling hungry, you're never more than a few steps away from a good meal.

• The Hawker Centre: Located right next to the main festive areas, the OTH Hawker Centre is the perfect pitstop. It offers a massive variety of halal and non-halal stalls, making it incredibly convenient for a quick dinner after catching a show or finishing a workout.

Final Verdict

Our Tampines Hub is the gold standard for community spaces. Between the festive CNY activities and the breezy, comfortable "arena" area, it’s a great place to spend an evening.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
I like Purple Panda because the staff are friendly, patient.

I haven’t tried their food yet, but I’ve heard good things about it, so I’m looking forward to giving it a try next time.

The environment at Our Tampines Hub is also really nice and lively where there a lot of chairs area . I like the hawker area a lot, especially the seafood stalls all the way at the back it has a good variety

Overall, it’s a chill place with a nice vibe, good service, and a lot of food options around.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Our Tampines Hub truly feels like the heartbeat of the community, and it’s easy to understand why it’s known as Singapore’s largest community and lifestyle centre. The scale is impressive, but more than that, it reflects Singapore’s resilience and commitment to supporting community living in a meaningful way.

The architecture is beautiful, modern, and thoughtfully designed with sustainability in mind. Everything is conveniently housed in one place — hawker centres, food courts, restaurants, learning centres, indoor and outdoor running tracks, rooftop gardens, and sky gardens. The layout makes it easy to spend hours here without feeling overwhelmed, whether you’re running errands or just hanging out.

One of the standout highlights is the large central screen with seating around it. It creates a fantastic shared atmosphere for concerts, movie screenings, or watching live-streamed matches, especially when Singapore is being represented at major events like the Olympics. There’s also a football court that regularly hosts matches and other events, adding to the vibrant energy of the space.

Families are well catered for too, with a climbing wall and dedicated play areas for children. The large auditorium, which seats around 1,500 people, is another impressive feature, often hosting performances and community events. Essential government services like Service SG are also located here, making it incredibly convenient. Even though it gets busy in the evenings, the lively footfall just shows how well-loved and actively used Our Tampines Hub really is.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
📍Level 2, Our Tampines Hub

👏🏻 Sheltered playground alert 👏🏻

Ah, this one holds a special place in our heart as my kid grew up at this playground. From stumbling around to confidently running full tilt at the trampoline now.

👧🏻 Toddler verdict: 8/10. The shelter is a big plus point in our tropical weather. This huge area is padded and comes complete with many toddler-friendly zones

👉 Parents’ note: There’s one more discreet (and sheltered) playground at level 4 if this one gets crowded

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I encountered an issue with my PayWave when tapping out at the gantry and approached the station staff for assistance. Unfortunately, my initial experience was unpleasant, as one of the staff members on duty was rude and showed a clear lack of patience.

I understand that it was already quite late, close to midnight, and it may have been a long shift. However, a poor attitude was not necessary, especially when assistance was being sought.

That said, I would like to express my sincere gratitude to Edward, who truly turned the situation around. He stepped in, assisted me promptly, and handled my queries with professionalism, patience, and kindness. His approach made all the difference and completely salvaged what could have been a very frustrating experience.

Edward is the kind of staff member every station should be proud of. He not only delivers excellent service but also restores confidence in the overall experience. He truly deserves to have a strong and supportive team behind him that reflects the same level of professionalism and care he demonstrates. We definitely need more people like Edward.

Because of Edward, I don’t feel it’s fair to give a low rating overall. While the earlier service was disappointing, Edward’s outstanding support deserves full recognition.

Once again, thank you Edward. You really saved the day.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you live in the East, Tampines Mall is likely your second home. As one of the most established neighbourhood malls in Singapore, it remains a powerhouse for shopping, dining, and community vibes. Here’s why it’s always buzzing:

• Location is King: It is perfectly situated right next to Tampines MRT (connecting both the East-West and Downtown lines) and the bus interchange. You can step off the train and be inside the mall in under a minute.

• The "Weekend Go-To": Whether you need to pick up groceries at NTUC FairPrice, grab new threads at Uniqlo, or catch the latest blockbuster at Golden Village, it’s the definitive weekend go-to place for shopping. It has that perfect mix of high-street brands and essential services.

• Festive Fairs & Atrium Events: One of the mall's highlights is its constant rotation of festive fairs at the Level 1 atrium. From massive Chinese New Year marketplaces (currently featuring the Year of the Horse 2026 treats!) to mid-autumn mooncake fairs and toys "R" us pop-ups, there’s always something new to see.

• Foodie Paradise: There are plenty of restaurants and shops to explore. From popular sit-down spots like Din Tai Fung and PUTIEN to quick bites at the basement food kiosks, you are truly spoilt for choice.

⚠️ A Word of Advice:

Be prepared—it gets incredibly crowded during weekends and public holidays. Since it’s the main hub for the Tampines residential heartland, finding a table at popular eateries during lunch or dinner can be a bit of a challenge. If you prefer a quieter stroll, weekday mornings are your best bet.

Despite the crowds, the vibrant energy and sheer convenience make every visit a pleasant one. It’s the heart of the neighbourhood for a reason!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
There are 3 plazas in Tampines, basically covering all the brands you’d normally need. The food options have also grown a lot—many more fragrance hotpot stalls, and even Lanzhou beef noodles have opened here.
🍜
It was a pity that Bedok Mall’s Charles & Keith and Haidilao closed down, but fortunately the stores here have always remained. Recently, quite a few new bubble tea shops have opened, and now almost all the major milk tea brands are gathered here, competing fiercely.
🧋
That rip‑off “Wildman gelato” shop has even opened a new branch here. Although they advertise free samples and have a buy‑one‑get‑one promotion after 9 p.m., the value is still very low! Their Chengdu branch was just as bad! No good impression at all.
🍦
The most popular store here is probably Don Don Donki, with delicious sweet potatoes 🍠 and various local‑style beef. You don’t need to travel all the way to Japan to enjoy Japanese food. Plus, they often have special promotions, making it a supermarket worth visiting regularly. Payment methods are also comprehensive.
🛒
In the first‑floor hall, there are often various bazaars, but the value for money isn’t always high. Some are even worse than the promotions inside BHG. Customers need to judge carefully—just because something is in the most prominent spot doesn’t mean it’s the best deal!
⚠️
Overall, Tampines Mall is quite good, with strong functionality. Residents living nearby are really fortunate.
😊

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Tampines Mall is one of the most convenient and well-rounded shopping malls in the east of Singapore. The location is excellent, as it is right beside Tampines MRT station and close to the bus interchange, making it very easy to visit by public transport.

The mall has a good mix of shops, food options, daily essentials, and lifestyle stores, so it is suitable for families, students, office workers, and residents nearby. Whether you need to do grocery shopping, buy clothes, grab a quick meal, have coffee, or just walk around, Tampines Mall has almost everything you need in one place.

I also like that the mall is easy to navigate. The layout is straightforward, and the shops are arranged in a way that makes it convenient to move around. There are many popular dining choices, from quick takeaway food to proper sit-down restaurants, which makes it a good place for both casual meals and family dining.

The mall is usually lively and busy, especially during weekends and meal times, but it still feels organised and comfortable. The environment is clean, air-conditioned, and well maintained. Toilets are generally clean, and there are also enough lifts and escalators to move between floors conveniently.

Overall, Tampines Mall is a reliable and convenient shopping mall with a good variety of retail, food, and services. It is a great place for everyday shopping, meals, and spending time with family or friends. Highly recommended for anyone staying in or visiting the Tampines area.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Tampines Mall is easy to go around — everything’s well-organized and convenient to find. Lots of food and shopping options, and it’s directly connected to MRT and bus interchange, so it’s really accessible!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Review of ✔️NLB Public Library Tampines Regional at Our Tampines Hub (OTH) 👍

On November 18, 2025, I had the opportunity to visit the large community centre, Our Tampines Hub (OTH), located in Tampines. During my visit, I explored the NLB Public Library Tampines Regional, which is conveniently situated inside the building.

I was pleased to find out that the library is quite spacious, spanning five levels from the 2nd to the 6th floor, and providing services for residents of all ages, from children to adults.

This venue is an excellent place for studying and will particularly appeal to book lovers. The library features numerous chairs and tables, ensuring ample seating at all times. It is an exceptionally quiet environment, making sounds from the children's section barely noticeable.

The indoor kids' playground is soundproof, allowing children to play freely. Parents can bring their children here with confidence, as staff are present to supervise and ensure their safety. Wonderful!

Members can also reserve seats in advance through an online booking system, with a maximum reservation period of four hours. Same-day reservations are available, except during busy times such as the start of school exams like the GCE O and A Levels, when the library becomes very crowded with students.

I thoroughly enjoyed my time there; it is a wonderful place to explore. We recently relocated to this area.

The residents of Tampines are indeed fortunate to have access to such a large, immensely popular library that is not only widely used but also deeply cherished.

Welcome to the Tampines Public Library!

Chris
21 Nov 2025

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Step into a space where curiosity thrives and knowledge knows no bounds. The Tampines Library isn’t just a building of books—it’s an inviting haven designed to spark learning and inspire deeper understanding of everything under the sky.
I dropped by on a weekday to look up some research and reference materials, and was amazed at how thoughtfully each level has been designed. Every corner draws you in, encouraging visitors to immerse themselves in discovery, reflection, and growth.
Whether you’re here for study, inspiration, or a quiet escape, this library offers a spectrum of possibilities waiting for you to explore.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
One of the best libraries in Singapore, hands down. This place is more than just a library it’s a full lifestyle and learning hub.
Spread across five levels, the space is huge, modern, and thoughtfully designed for everyone from kids to working adults. The environment is clean, comfortable, and perfect whether you want to study, read, or just relax.

What really impressed me is the variety of facilities from quiet study areas and cosy reading corners to tech spaces like makerspaces and even an indoor playground for kids. It truly caters to all ages and interests.

The book collection is extensive, and the self-service borrowing and return system is super efficient and easy to use. Many spots also offer great views of the surrounding stadium, making the whole experience even more enjoyable.

Only downside is that it can get quite crowded during weekends, but that just shows how popular and well-loved this place is.

Highly recommended if you’re looking for a productive study spot, a peaceful escape, or even a place to bring your family

review_text:
Tampines Library is located on the second floor of Tampines Hub. One thing I noticed is that it has two entrances – one of them is nearer to the eateries, which is super convenient if you want to grab a quick bite before or after studying.

The study corner is a bit limited in terms of seating, but almost all seats come with a power plug, which is really handy. The library is also well maintained and clean. Overall, a good place to study if you can grab a spot early.

review_text: Kembangan MRT is a gem for residents of Mill Creek Condo, just a 5-minute walk away via a sheltered linkway, making it convenient rain or shine. The nearby bus stop offers direct services to the airport and city, enhancing connectivity. The station is well-lit from night to dawn, ensuring safety. place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I would like to extend my sincere commendation to Mr Ahmed and his team for their professionalism and compassion.

Earlier today, I encountered an elderly gentleman who appeared disoriented and was asking for directions. I suspected he may have been living with dementia and accompanied him to the MRT for assistance. Mr Ahmed was able to converse with the elderly gentleman in dialect, which greatly helped to calm and reassure him. Together with his team, he handled the situation with patience, empathy, and professionalism.

Their calm approach and respectful handling of the situation left a strong impression on me. I truly hope the elderly gentleman managed to find his way home safely.

Thank you to Mr Ahmed and the team for exemplifying kindness, professionalism, and community spirit.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My child often happily tells us, "Teacher taught me a new song today," or "I made some great friends at school."

From these words, I can clearly feel his love for MindChamps.

Ms. Hong Wei Leng, the English teacher, is gentle and patient. My child speaks Chinese, and Ms. He is like a mother to me, reminding me to drink water every day and showing her deep concern for us. This school offers rich content and guides children to learn and grow in a joyful way. I am very grateful to the school and teachers for their dedication, which makes my child look forward to every day.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Repeat customer here. My elder child was at Mindchamps Bedok from 2018-2021. My second child has been with them since 2023. There has been some staff changes over the years but the quality of care and teaching have been maintained at a high standard. My kids have thrived there. The staff team is great - very caring and truly invested in the kids' wellbeing.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: The school is very beautiful, and we had a wonderful experience with the school manager. I felt very happy bringing my child for a visit, and if there is an opportunity, we will definitely enroll our child here.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: We had a wonderful experience with MindChamps Bedok! The teachers and staff are caring, dedicated, and always create a positive environment for the children. A special shoutout for the amazing Champions Day sports event — it was so well organised, fun-filled, and truly memorable for both kids and parents. The activities were engaging, energetic, and brought out great teamwork and confidence in the children. Thank you to the team for making it such a successful and enjoyable day!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Choosing the right preschool for our son was really important to us, and we’re very grateful for the experience he has had here. The teachers are incredibly dedicated and genuinely care about the children. They take the time to understand each child and create a supportive environment where the kids feel comfortable and encouraged to learn.

We’ve also really appreciated the variety of activities the school organizes, which keep the children engaged and excited about coming to school. One of the most noticeable improvements we’ve seen is in our son’s speech. Since joining the preschool, his confidence in communicating has grown tremendously and his speech has improved significantly.

Overall, we’re very thankful for the care, dedication, and effort the teachers put into the children every day. Special shout out to Ms Liya, Zhang Laoshi, Ms Eve, Ms Ros, Ms Zee for getting my son to settle in to school nicely ❤️

review_text: Many thanks to Ms. Anni ASM of SIGLAP MRT STN staff. Because it was raining tonight, She kindly lent me an umbrella and service was excellent.Very professional.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: This mrt station was the nearest to East Coast park food lagoons. Walking distance and easy for cycling too.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: This MRT doesn't have too much to speak of on its own. There is a nearby Mosque for any Muslims who live around here and there are a few places to eat but you do need to walk to East Coast Road though. The Main highlight in my Opinion is Bus 36's Exclusive Bus Stop at Siglap Link somewhere Behind the MRT. Until the Changi Airport Expansion of the Thomson East Coast Line Arrives, this is By far the Fastest route to go to Airport. The only criticism I have is it's not clear enough where this Bus Stop (And Siglap Link) are. When I came here, I tried to use the Route to Airport I mentioned previously but accidentally went to the Wrong exit towards Marine Parade Road. So a Solution would be Indicating more clearly where Siglap Link Is (I only found one near the Escalator to Siglap Link).

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Nice station, easy and clear direction everywhere. Walking distance to east coast park.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
As a visiting tourist,
The MRT system of Singapore is the best and so easy to understand and navigate around the city

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Vivocity shopping mall is located at harborfront mrt station.inside vivocity there is harbourfront centre.therefore vivocity is a shopping mall with lots of food n shopping.there is even a library inside the mall.there r 2 kfc,2 mcdonalds in this mall one at vivo another at centre.jollibee,all American food, burger king also at this mall.2 foodcourt also 1 at vivo n 1 at centre.lots of food options(halal n non halal),many snack shops, flavored drinks,many clothes shop,popular bookstore, jewellery shops,fairprice Xtra,cold storage,7 eleven,cheers all on this mall.bread shops,pizza hut also at this mall.from this mall take a monorail at the top floor to go to Sentosa.from inside vivo mall head out n u could c a beautiful sea view.i have many times seen the sea view.from vivo mall can c lots of amazing views outside.i always spend lots of time in vivo when I come harbourfront.every1 should visit this place.every1 would definitely love it.💞 I travel to lots of places ... Eat lots of different foods... Love sharing my experience with everyone so m posting lots of google reviews ... Hope everyone loves it ... 😁

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A crowded megamall in Singapore. Can go there directly by MRT,  very convenient! The malls have so many shoplots, restaurants and cafes. A lot of branded shops can be found at here.  You can go Sentosa Singapore from here too. There is a park at rooftop as well, very chilling and relaxing. Now they have new year decoration right outside of the entrance, very attractive!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Really enjoyed visiting VivoCity 🛍️
The mall is HUGE and has pretty much everything you need. Lots of shopping, food options, cafes, and entertainment all in one place. It’s also super convenient since it connects directly to Sentosa, which makes it a great stop before or after exploring the island 🌴🚆
One of my favorite parts was definitely the terrace area 😍 The outdoor space was so pretty and relaxing, especially with the open view, greenery, and waterfront atmosphere. It felt really nice to just sit there for a while and enjoy the vibe, especially around sunset 🌅✨ Such a nice contrast from the busy indoor mall area.
The mall itself was clean, spacious, and easy to walk around despite how big it is. I also liked that there were plenty of seating areas to rest between shopping and exploring. Overall, VivoCity felt more than just a shopping mall, it was honestly a really enjoyable place to spend a few hours 🤍

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This mega mall that serves the residents in the west side of Singapore,  has upgraded recently with the introduction of more eateries at the basement, when one exits from the mrt station. Singapore is a foodie haven , so it is important to pump in popular brands to draw the hungry working people n tourists.  One can see that every outlet is packed during meal time. So it is very competitive for the shops to come up with promotional set menu to attract customers.  Another local pastime is shopping, so sale events are usually held at the front podium area. The mall houses two floors for those who enjoy shopping.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
So many stores to choose from, this mall is huge!
The Christmas Market was great, it was lit nicely and there were a lot of good Christmas shopping options like baked goods, wrapping paper, Christmas signs and cards.
Everything was very clean but since its a huge mall it does get very very busy.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
The market and food centre is named after the Archipelago Brewery Company, one of Singapore's oldest breweries (the ABC part), and the brick making factories, which once operated in the area.

The market and food centre was established in the 1970s to centralise street hawkers and to provide affordable meals to the residents and workers in the now bustling and rapidly industrialising Bukit Merah and Queenstown estates.

Over the years, it has grown from a humble neighbourhood spot into a culinary hub famous for its heritage operators and recipes passed down through generations.

Today, it boasts two Michelin Bib Gourmand stalls serving Chinese cuisine :
- Ah Er Soup
- Jason Penang Cuisine

There was a fried Hokkien mee stall which once was also of Bib Gourmand status, but is now closed when its hawker retired.

There is also a bak chor mee stall holding a Michelin Selected status.

Usually it is very crowded on weekdays. We were there on a weekend, and it is easier to get a table. Quite a number of stalls were closed but there are still many highly recommended stalls open.

Date of visit : June 2026

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This food market offers a good variety of dishes, and several stalls are worth trying. The steam fish was especially fresh and flavorful. The BBQ pork rice came with tender, well-cooked meat that made the dish satisfying. The wan ton mee was not bad overall, though the wan ton itself can be skipped—especially since the dumplings were sold out during my visit.

There’s also a freshly baked bread stall that serves a lovely muffin, definitely a nice treat to end the meal.

Overall, a pleasant stop with a few standout items worth returning for.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Came here for the 505 White Carrot Cake. Personally not a big cai tao kway eater but so far this particular stall has been delivering great taste and flavor! Introduced by my partner who is a huge fan. It's pretty much the only white carrot cake I eat tbh. Followed them from jurong west till here hahaha.

After moving to this new loc, seems like it got crispier (as it should!! I do love that) and their chilli had an upgrade 😋

Priced at $4/5/6 as of today, it's definitely value for money.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: First visited to this market. Tried the traditional Rojak which was prepared by two seniors lady and taste was really good and realised that with 4 SGD, few slides of apples were found whereas it is hardly found from others similar store. Love it👍

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: ABC BRICKWORKS Have many good hawker stalls with different varities of Chinese, Indian & Muslim stalls providing choice of food

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
On 1st January 2026, I took the last bus service number 14 from Bedok Interchange. The bus driver was a Malaysian Indian.

I am in my 70s and with a walking stick with lights blinking as an indicator to avoid another accident after my recovery from spine injury.

The particular driver drove out very fast and recklessly from the interchange exit.

He started to drive proper only after picking up passengers along East Coast Road.

At some bus stops he deliberately stopped and waited with pretext of picking up passengers and continued with his journey.

Along the way the bus kept jerking as he drives recklessly.

On 2nd January 2026, I bordered the bus service number 14 at about 6+ pm along East Coast Road nearby Siglap Centre to go to Heart Beat @ActiveSG at   11 Bedok North Street 1.

Unfortunately the same Indian driver was driving the bus service number 14.

I then politely greeted him and went to my seat. The driver did not wait until I completely get seated.

Just before the traffic junctions at Heart Beat ActiveSG, I pressed the bell to alight at the bus stop beside Heart Beat.

During this time the traffic lights changed to red and the bus stopped at the traffic junction.

When arrived at the bus stop the driver did not stop to alight me.

I then pressed again the bell before of the coffee shop at Blk 21 Bedok North St 1

I walked towards the bus driver with my walking stick and ask him as to why he didn’t stop bus at the bus stop beside Heart Beat.

He then claimed that I only pressed the bell after the bus-stop. He did not realise that I pressed the bell the second time to check whether it’s working proper.

He ask me to walk back to my seat while he was driving fast. He then advised me to walk back myself Heart Beat from the bus interchange.

I politely asked him in Tamil to be nice to old forks in Singapore. He then directed me to make a complaint to the office at the bus interchange.

Subsequently, I made a complaint to the officer at the bus interchange and given him my contact number for any further clarification.

I had taken many buses in the past and all drivers were very polite and helpful. They usually patiently wait for me to completely get seated before driving off again and make sure I had safely alight from the bus before driving off again.

I am making this feedback for public interest for our old folks in Singapore and am not against any individuals.o

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Bedok interchange has always been known as a vibrant place! Lots of shops example : coffee shops, cafes, restaurant, salons, supermarket and others.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Thanks Mr Jack Leong for ur proactive service n helping up just now.
Wanting to activate my senior card n he was very helpful approached me n asked if he could help.
Office closed on Sunday
Will come back again.

But thanks to Mr Leong for ur help. He speaks very fluent English n he’s very fit too walking up n down helping pp in doubt.

Wish you a blessed 2025 Mr Jack Leong
GBU

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Mass scale bus interchange, a lot of buses turnover every minute. If em not overstating there is bus coming in or out ever 3 to 5 seconds. AC is good, always being cleaned by dedicated staff, still a lot of people leave empty cans n garbage on way or on benches, seen people eating sandwiches while in the queue, however it is not advisable.
Well for the architecture it is a marvel, bus going in n coming out of different gates while in same building you will luxury flats above. So it's a bus depot below the apartment building. Nevertheless, at the basement if same venue there lies a mall full of activities and shops yet clean.
Way out of Bus interchange you can find dozen of food outlets n stores leading towards the MRT stations that makes it a duo point.
A must visit place for tourists Visiting Singapore.

review_text: Bedok Mall is well located beside Bedok MRT, Bedok Bus Interchange and the famous Bedok Interchange Hawker Center.  Many commuters usually like to drop by the Bedok Mall for essential shopping.  B1 and B2 mainly retail and F&B stalls.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Bedok Mall is the vibrant heart of the East, offering an incredibly convenient and comprehensive shopping experience for both locals and visitors. What I love most about this mall is its seamless integration with the Bedok MRT station and the bus interchange, making it exceptionally easy to access. Despite its compact size compared to downtown malls, it is packed with a fantastic variety of retail shops, supermarkets, and dining options that cater to every taste and budget. It has a wonderful community feel—it’s always bustling with energy but remains very family-friendly and well-organized. Whether you are looking for trendy fashion, quick daily necessities at the supermarket, or a great meal at one of the many popular restaurants, Bedok Mall has it all under one roof. The layout is intuitive, making it a breeze to navigate even during the busy weekend rush. It’s truly a one-stop destination that perfectly serves the needs of the neighborhood. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a convenient and fulfilling shopping trip in the East of Singapore!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Heartbeat@Bedok is more than just a building; it’s a beautifully designed, integrated lifestyle hub that truly feels like the pulse of the neighborhood. Whether you're there for a workout, a meal, or just to pass the time, it offers a welcoming atmosphere for all ages.

What Makes it Special:

• Airy & Spacious Design: The architecture is a standout. It features high ceilings and clever natural ventilation that keeps the entire complex feeling breezy and airy, even on warmer days. The lush greenery integrated into the terraces makes it feel like an urban forest.

• A Foodie’s Haven: You’ll never go hungry here. There are lots of restaurants and cafes ranging from popular chains like Sushi Express and Burger King to the One Two Eat food court, which serves up a wide variety of local and international cuisines.

• Plenty of Seating: One of the best parts about this hub is the abundance of public space. There are plenty of seats scattered throughout the levels, especially near the library and community areas, making it easy to find a spot to rest.

• Stay Active: For those looking for some friendly competition, there are table tennis tables (TTX) available for public use. It’s a great way for people who want to play a quick game to engage with others in the community.

• Community & Creativity: The building houses a large Community Centre and a modern Public Library. There’s even a dedicated handicraft corner and various interest group rooms where residents can pick up new skills or work on DIY projects.

👵 The Perfect Senior Hangout
Heartbeat@Bedok is arguably one of the best places for the elderlies to hangout. With the senior care center and polyclinic located on-site, the environment is very senior-friendly (ramps, elevators, and large-print books in the library). It’s common to see groups of seniors having a chat over coffee or sitting in the airy common areas, making it a very inclusive and social space.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Had a great time at the Singapore Police Force Community Roadshow 2025 @ Bedok! 🎉 It's clear why I'm quite keen on visiting Heartbeat @ Bedok—it was the place to be for this exciting event.
I was so glad I decided to visit Heartbeat @ Bedok today! Not only is it a great community hub, but it was also the venue of the FINAL DAY of the fantastic Police Community Roadshow (PCR) 2025.

The atmosphere was buzzing with energy! The highlight for me was definitely the fantastic line-up of activities:
• 🎶 Singapore Police Force Band Performance: I got to catch the amazing Police Force Band, which was truly captivating! (The video I took shows the "Bringing Beat to You Since 1925" segment on the screen!)
• 🐕 Thrilling Police K-9 Show: The Police K-9 Show was impressive, demonstrating the skill and discipline of our police dogs and their handlers.
• 🚨 Exclusive Collectible: The main reason I was so excited was that I successfully registered to redeem a Die-Cast Police Vehicle! It's an awesome collectible and a great souvenir from the event.

It was a wonderful opportunity to see the Police Force engage with the community and showcase their work. Events like this make Heartbeat @ Bedok a truly vibrant and special place to visit!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: I really loved the place, especially the library. Many amenities for example diverse food options (including local and non-local) as well as fitness, leisure, social. Library was quiet enough for reading and studying, would recommend the lower floor for studying, and also finally I have found a piano in better working condition compared to others outside which are usually busted due to kids banging on it or it being ancient. Would highly recommend going there if you want to have fun and/or destress.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: A well laid out community club with social facilities like medical , food , library and recreation needs for the Bedok residences. Events are organised on the ground floor podium where is visible and arouse public interest. A good way to promote the club  n sports activities to people who walk by this place. To attract more people to the hub, centre groups can hold more community programmes.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Beautifully built architecture and very spacious vicinity. Ideally made for the community center point. This really represents as heartbeat of Bedok.
Toilets are clean, escalators n lifts properly working. Food courts and community offices are also there. Nearby mall open melarkets and a fair price mall adds value to the proximity and made it real heartbeat of town.
A must visit place to understand how the local communities are living well organized and with mutual coordination here.
They have placed a temporary Library to read and share books within the community. Also they organize movies screening on an open projector screen with a very good sound and video quality for free.

• 🐕 Thrilling Police K-9 Show: The Police K-9 Show was impressive, demonstrating the skill and discipline of our police dogs and their handlers.
• 🚨 Exclusive Collectible: The main reason I was so excited was that I successfully registered to redeem a Die-Cast Police Vehicle! It's an awesome collectible and a great souvenir from the event.

It was a wonderful opportunity to see the Police Force engage with the community and showcase their work. Events like this make Heartbeat @ Bedok a truly vibrant and special place to visit!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: I really loved the place, especially the library. Many amenities for example diverse food options (including local and non-local) as well as fitness, leisure, social. Library was quiet enough for reading and studying, would recommend the lower floor for studying, and also finally I have found a piano in better working condition compared to others outside which are usually busted due to kids banging on it or it being ancient. Would highly recommend going there if you want to have fun and/or destress.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: A well laid out community club with social facilities like medical , food , library and recreation needs for the Bedok residences. Events are organised on the ground floor podium where is visible and arouse public interest. A good way to promote the club  n sports activities to people who walk by this place. To attract more people to the hub, centre groups can hold more community programmes.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Beautifully built architecture and very spacious vicinity. Ideally made for the community center point. This really represents as heartbeat of Bedok.
Toilets are clean, escalators n lifts properly working. Food courts and community offices are also there. Nearby mall open melarkets and a fair price mall adds value to the proximity and made it real heartbeat of town.
A must visit place to understand how the local communities are living well organized and with mutual coordination here.
They have placed a temporary Library to read and share books within the community. Also they organize movies screening on an open projector screen with a very good sound and video quality for free.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Very impolite smrt staff. Doesn’t have small change to top up my EZ-Link card hence I asked if he can change with me but he said there isn’t any small change for exchange. Any other mrt stations I went to they were all willing to change with me as it is for topping up of card purposes. In the end I missed my bus and was late for work. Thumbs up and good job to this staff!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: It's one of the deepest mrt station. Might be as deep as newton. I like the spaciousness and the long flight of escalator up to the exit. The view outside is also nice and spacious. At exit B, you can see a mosque nearby. Liked the view inside and outside as we seldom come here.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:

Must visit place in Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

review_text:
Refurbished recently. Some nice deco outside but not much has changed.
The net playground is still there, which is a good thing and a unique attraction to this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
We visited IMM Building, Singapore’s largest outlet mall, and had a good shopping experience. This place really lives up to what many reviewers say — it has all varieties from high-end brands to low-end shops, making it easier to find what you need under one roof. You can discover everything from fashion and sportswear to electronics, home goods, and plenty of dining options.

Many visitors also love the discounts on big brands (up to around 80% at outlet stores), and the mall is spacious and easy to navigate. There are lots of food and beverage outlets to take a break while shopping.

Crowds can get heavier on weekends, and some items in outlet stores may not be the latest season stock, but overall it’s a great place for bargain hunting and casual shopping.

Overall experience was pleasant — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

Hum log IMM Building gaye aur overall shopping experience kaafi achhi rahi. Jaise online reviews mein bhi log likhte hain, yahan high-end se leke low-end tak sab varieties mil jaati hain, jis se shopping easy ho jati hai — fashion, sportswear, electronics, home goods aur bahut saare food options sab ek hi jagah par.

Bohot saare visitors ko big brands par ache discounts bhi milte hain aur mall ka layout kaafi spacious hai jisme easily explore kar sakte ho.

Weekend par thoda crowd zyada ho sakta hai aur outlet stores mein sab items latest season ke nahi milte, lekin overall yeh mall bargain hunting aur casual shopping ke liye ek achhi jagah hai.

Overall experience achha raha — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Singapore's only outlet mall with all major brands under one roof. You will get good deals from top brands here. Don't forget to plan to visit here when you travel to Singapore. Must visit mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
IMM Mall is a great spot for shopping, especially if you’re looking for outlet deals. It has a wide range of stores, including brands like Coach, Michael Kors, Kate Spade, Charles & Keith, Pedro, Cotton On, and plenty of footwear outlets.
Getting there is easy—take the MRT to Jurong East MRT Station and follow the signs. It’s about a 10 minute walk, and you’ll pass JEM on the way.
There are also lots of dining options, making it a good place to spend a few hours. If you’re shopping, if you spend over SGD 100 in a store, you can request a GST refund receipt—just show your passport at the time of purchase. You can then claim the refund at the airport during departure.
Overall, a solid choice if you’re planning a shopping trip in Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
For my family and me living in the west, IMM is one of our our go-to spots for shopping, leisure, and meals because it is just so convenient.
Also, being directly connected to Jurong East MRT and the Bus Interchange makes it exceptionally accessible.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Have visited IMM since I was a kid. That was like decades ago. Since then, new wings were constructed, parking has expanded, new links to the Jurong shopping cluster has been built and stores have come and go. Really like how there everything here, from daily necessities to outlet stores. Weekend parking is a nightmare though and the gantry should be upgraded to reduce the jam at the carpark entrance. Also, glad that level 3 is pet friendly thanks to the new BYD cafe.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
This review is for the counter staff.

Asked the trainee staff - Eunice Koh to change my 78 year old father s mobile to mine on his SINGPASS. It was not done properly because it still prompt for his face verification instead of OTP (to my mobile), she kept insisting me to call the singpass helpline instead, and that she does not know why face verification is essential.

Of course it's needed because the login was done via my phone and the update of mobile is not successful.
I insisted to her the number was not updated then she do the update again, finally it's done properly.

Then I said I want to change the password so I can use my own password instead of whatever my dad used to. Again, she direct me to singpass helpline?! Is her role there just to deflect responsibilities to the technical helpline? Because I know she jolly could do it on the spot! She did eventually, only after I questioned her!

If the state wants to digitalize everything including seniors who has zero digital knowledge, make sure you hire competent staff to help. I know she s a trainee, but really does not take much for one to have some common sense.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
NEX is a big, convenient mall that’s hard to beat for errands or a casual day out. It’s right by Serangoon MRT and the bus interchange, so getting in and out is easy. Inside, it’s bright, clean, and well-air-conditioned; even on weekends when it’s busy, the walkways are wide enough that you can still move comfortably.

Food options are plentiful, everything from quick bites to sit-down spots, and the food court is efficient with staff constantly wiping tables, so turnover is fast. Shopping is just as varied, with fashion, beauty, tech, and a large supermarket, which makes it great for one-stop “get everything done” runs. Families will appreciate the clear signage and clean restrooms, plus plenty of places to take a break if you’re with kids or older folks. Overall, a reliable place to eat, shop, and cool off, practical for a lunch stop and pleasant enough for an unhurried weekend stroll.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Huge shopping mall connected to Serangoon MRT. Has all outlets that you need for shopping and food.
Very accessible. Can get a bit crowded on weekends.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Probably the busiest, most bustling mall in the heartlands. It’s a decent size with a pretty good tenant mix, but word on the street is that the old Isetan spot is being turned into a bunch of boring tuition centres and a Kiztopia. If that’s true, we’ll probably be visiting a lot less.

There’s also been a massive proliferation of PRC restaurants in recent years, which makes us even less inclined to frequent the place. We might drop by when Chin Mee Chin finally opens, but we aren’t going to visit as much as before.

P.S The elevators are always under maintenance or not working.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Good Location Shopping!!!
But saw such behaviours of youngsters is totally embarrassing and flabergusted, giving others no place to walk via bulldozing in a row.
Saw them few times doing this in 2025 and 2 times in 2026. Video was captured when I changed my handphone and was trying out it's functions.
The worst is, this group have been doing this bulldozing walks everywhere they go, even Tourists has been complaining on TikTok regarding such behaviours when they visited Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The management of NEX mall (Serangoon MRT) must be fast asleep. A couple of their escalators (to L2 & B1, see photos) have been out of service for quite a few months and no repair work has been initiated. These escalators are covered with cladding and nobody has been spotted working on them! Rather than have them fixed, signs are instead posted to direct customers to other (working, for the time being) escalators. This smacks of apathy and a pathetic lack of professional pride & customer care. Whoever is at the top needs to deal with it - unless, of course, he/she/they also can’t be bothered. In which case, the problem is endemic.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Here's a review you can use:

---

Ryan chou
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Woodlands Waterfront Park is one of my favorite spots to unwind. The park offers a beautiful, relaxing view of the Johor Strait, especially during sunset. The waterfront promenade is perfect for a peaceful stroll or a bike ride, and there’s plenty of open space for picnics with family and friends.

I also love the iconic jetty—it’s a great place to take in the sea breeze and watch the boats go by. The park is well-maintained, with clean paths and plenty of benches to sit and enjoy the scenery. Highly recommended for anyone looking to escape the city hustle without going too far!

There is some bus stop near here but you can also walk here from woodlands mrt throguh Admiralty park. Not many facilities here except for the seafood restaurant and just greenery around.

The lor mee is really nice with crispy fish! Can taste the lor sauce. BCM is also good and I love the otah HAHA it's $3 for 6, that's basically 2010 prices and has a nice char. Carrot cake is crispy with thick egg omelette too if you're into that :)

And of course the tang yuan is good!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Very big mall next to stadium. Good for concert go-ers and sports events. Sells mainly food. Carpark is huge. Walking distance to MRT and Kallang Leisure Park. Easy to find food during lunch. Nothing much for tourists to sight seeing here. Mall is very clean and spacious to walk. With Starbucks, Chi Cha bubble tea, Fun Tea (for some local kopi n delights). No fast food in this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Great place for a meal. Lunch/dinner/breakfast. It’s a great place for kids to play nearby concert venues and very convenient like outside and MRT station exit.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: I like this mall. It isn't a typical shopping mall where one goes to shop. This mall caters to sports enthusiasts given its location. It is a great after-sports place to go to relax, wash up and eat. The walking area around the mall is very spacious. It is a mere minute walk away from pthe Stadium MRT station and for those who drives, ample parking.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
A nice, vibrant place with a good mix of shopping, dining, and activities. It's conveniently located next to the Stadium MRT, making it easily accessible. The mall has a relaxed and spacious atmosphere, and it’s never too crowded, which makes walking around very comfortable.

There are a variety of stores to explore, from daily essentials to fashion outlets. Dining options are plenty, with cafes, restaurants, and a food court offering many choices. There’s also Climb Central for indoor rock climbing and Splash-N-Surf for kids, making it great for families and those looking for active fun.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Love this lib. Looks so new and design so contemporary. As with all the new libraries, the design is thoughtful with good reading areas, working areas, charging points. These days I love going to the library because it is such an enjoyment experience. Nestled within heartbeat@bedok with supermarket and f&b. Building has its own car park. Water dispenser avail just outside lib. Lib spread two floors with internal staircase.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I was surprised to find tourist reviews for such a niche place! This must be the kind of spot people visit only if they’re on an extended trip to Singapore and have the time to explore a community library with no real scenic appeal.

The library is located on the 2nd and 3rd floors of the community center (CC). Taking the escalator is much faster since the cylindrical lift is extremely inefficient—you might have to wait up to 5 minutes for a ride! The upper floors also host other activity facilities, so even on weekdays, there are quite a few people queuing for the lift.

The overall environment here is definitely not as quiet as a university library. You’ll encounter a mix of people, including uncles whose phones aren’t on silent mode and those who talk loudly on their phones.

The 3rd floor is even noisier because it houses the children’s reading area. The noise from kids running around often drifts down to the 2nd floor, making it less peaceful as well.

Sofa seating is almost always occupied—even if there’s no one sitting, personal belongings will be placed there to reserve the spot. There are usually available seats in the hard plastic chair area, though.

If you plan to spend a long time studying or reading here, you might want to bring a long-sleeved coat, as the air conditioning is set to 18°C. For residents dealing with noisy construction at home or in public spaces, this library can be a good escape. Although it’s not particularly quiet, it’s much more peaceful compared to the noise pollution in residential buildings or on the streets.

For visitors/tourists looking to take aesthetic photos, this might not be the best choice. The design of the bookshelves here isn’t as visually appealing as those in the Orchard Road Library. However, if you’re out of places to visit and just want to relax, this spot offers a glimpse into the daily lives of locals and their community atmosphere.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I visited the NLB Bedok library today to use one of the public PCs. While I was using it, the computer froze and I encountered some difficulties. I sought assistance from the staff, Miss Norsiah , who responded immediately and resolved the issue confidently and efficiently.
I would like to take this opportunity to express my sincere appreciation and thank her for her prompt, professional, and excellent assistance. Her helpfulness made my visit a pleasant experience. Once again, many thanks.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Bedok Public Library staffs is extremely kind and willing to help. It is also we'll designed from interior to exterior. I liked  the library's Teens Study and Reading Area,Early Literary Area,Multimedia Stations area and Magazine Reading Area the most I would strongly encourage you to visit this Library if you want to get BookBugs 3rd release cards as I got 35 Buwan,54 Sisik and 57 Liraes here with only 85 times.The BookBugs dispensing machine and stamp booth is at Level 3 of the librarynear the borrowing station (At level 3 main Entrance).It is also very convenient as it is at Heartbeat@Bedok, which is just 10 mins walk from EW5 Bedok station and there is lift service from level 2 to level 3 if you want to go to the early Literacy area (level 3) from the adult area (level 2) .I would like to visit this library again.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Clean and conducive atmosphere for reading and studying. Most of the spots were occupied by students who seem to be camping there , and elderly reading news paper. As a first time visitor, there are few staff to assist and level 2 entrance were closed. An impressive catalog of books (including other foreign languages ones). There seem to be seminar rooms for special program .
