I liked  the library's Teens Study and Reading Area,Early Literary Area,Multimedia Stations area and Magazine Reading Area the most I would strongly encourage you to visit this Library if you want to get BookBugs 3rd release cards as I got 35 Buwan,54 Sisik and 57 Liraes here with only 85 times.The BookBugs dispensing machine and stamp booth is at Level 3 of the librarynear the borrowing station (At level 3 main Entrance).It is also very convenient as it is at Heartbeat@Bedok, which is just 10 mins walk from EW5 Bedok station and there is lift service from level 2 to level 3 if you want to go to the early Literacy area (level 3) from the adult area (level 2) .I would like to visit this library again.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Clean and conducive atmosphere for reading and studying. Most of the spots were occupied by students who seem to be camping there , and elderly reading news paper. As a first time visitor, there are few staff to assist and level 2 entrance were closed. An impressive catalog of books (including other foreign languages ones). There seem to be seminar rooms for special program .

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Love this lib. Looks so new and design so contemporary. As with all the new libraries, the design is thoughtful with good reading areas, working areas, charging points. These days I love going to the library because it is such an enjoyment experience. Nestled within heartbeat@bedok with supermarket and f&b. Building has its own car park. Water dispenser avail just outside lib. Lib spread two floors with internal staircase.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I was surprised to find tourist reviews for such a niche place! This must be the kind of spot people visit only if they’re on an extended trip to Singapore and have the time to explore a community library with no real scenic appeal.

The library is located on the 2nd and 3rd floors of the community center (CC). Taking the escalator is much faster since the cylindrical lift is extremely inefficient—you might have to wait up to 5 minutes for a ride! The upper floors also host other activity facilities, so even on weekdays, there are quite a few people queuing for the lift.

The overall environment here is definitely not as quiet as a university library. You’ll encounter a mix of people, including uncles whose phones aren’t on silent mode and those who talk loudly on their phones.

The 3rd floor is even noisier because it houses the children’s reading area. The noise from kids running around often drifts down to the 2nd floor, making it less peaceful as well.

Sofa seating is almost always occupied—even if there’s no one sitting, personal belongings will be placed there to reserve the spot. There are usually available seats in the hard plastic chair area, though.

If you plan to spend a long time studying or reading here, you might want to bring a long-sleeved coat, as the air conditioning is set to 18°C. For residents dealing with noisy construction at home or in public spaces, this library can be a good escape. Although it’s not particularly quiet, it’s much more peaceful compared to the noise pollution in residential buildings or on the streets.

For visitors/tourists looking to take aesthetic photos, this might not be the best choice. The design of the bookshelves here isn’t as visually appealing as those in the Orchard Road Library. However, if you’re out of places to visit and just want to relax, this spot offers a glimpse into the daily lives of locals and their community atmosphere.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I visited the NLB Bedok library today to use one of the public PCs. While I was using it, the computer froze and I encountered some difficulties. I sought assistance from the staff, Miss Norsiah , who responded immediately and resolved the issue confidently and efficiently.
I would like to take this opportunity to express my sincere appreciation and thank her for her prompt, professional, and excellent assistance. Her helpfulness made my visit a pleasant experience. Once again, many thanks.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Incident Report – MRT Train (Paya Lebar to Bedok)
Date: 28 October 2025
Time: Around 10:00 AM

This morning, at about 10:00 AM, I boarded the train at Paya Lebar station. I had a team meeting to attend and was required to take the minutes. Unfortunately, my earpiece was damaged after I dropped it earlier, so I could only play the meeting audio at the lowest possible volume.

While I was trying to join the meeting quietly, a man sitting two seats beside me suddenly shouted at me. I explained to him softly that I had already turned the volume to the lowest setting. However, he continued to raise his voice and scold me.

Before alighting at the next station at Bedok, he continued shouting. I had to log out of my meeting and ended up missing an important discussion. As he was about to leave, I noticed him turning sideways and taking several photos of me . I could see that his phone camera was on, and he appeared to be snapping my picture repeatedly without my consent. I already log of the meeting he still continuously want to find problem !

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: 3+ level mall in the east of Singapore. Easy access from the train station. Walk down across and up from the station exit. Lots of food and retail shops. Also some outdoor traditional hawker stalls next door.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Bedok MRT station is spacious, clean and good air ventilation. It is a busy station and located next to shopping mall and hawker centre. Numerous buses also pass by this station.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: A busy station surrounded by various amenities, such as a shopping mall, hawker centre, town square, and others. Airy design makes it well ventilated, which is a strong point in Singapore's hot weather.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
This isn’t a review on the transport itself, it’s about my recent experience with one of the security at the station.

I had lunch at Bedok Mall and I wanted to head back to the airport via the MRT and my sister & I were stopped by the security/auxiliary police for a “random” luggage check. It didn’t seem random at the time because I saw there were other people bringing luggages and they weren’t stopped. Both of us felt targeted as if we did a crime. I have always enjoyed coming to Singapore and I’m always here for concerts and most recently the Grand Prix but this experience made me feel that this is racial profiling. The security of entering/exiting the country is so strict so what of crime could we possibly commit?

I’d still come to Singapore but maybe not stop by the neighbourhood areas to avoid being targeted again.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Still having the authentic Kampong feel. Drinks tend to be on the sweet side. Sort've a Malaysia feel to it.

The place used to have about a fourth of the shops occupied by hawkers selling Chinese dishes. Recently though, many of those shops were taken over by Malay hawkers, to sell those same 'chinese' dishes.

Is that a monument to integration, or a monument to self segregation though? Not too sure, something to think about.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I personally like this place as there are only local food with local price, amazingly enjoyable. We got roasted chicken rice, teh alia, sugar cane juice and kuay teow which were all so good.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Fabulous food and the best stalls are the ones with long queues such as the halal kway teow and Hokkien noodles and the satay! And all with the breeze from the seaside makes this dining experience totally enjoyable! And the teh tarik is amazing!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
1st time visiting this food centre, a lot of halal options, didn't try all the shops, tried the mee rebus with Satay, taste wise about blant for both the stay gravy and mee rebus gravy.
Epok Epok shop mee Soto was awesome, I found the gravy a bit on the salty side maybe due to my taste buds, epok epok was fresh and hot but found the flour a bit hard to break, felt more like biscuit. Ccarrot cake was ok, bought back the wanton noodle to eat, taste wise was normal.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Almost all stalls sell some form of satay dish. As always, tables almost full of patrons. Not wheelchair friendly place. But quality & selection of food had vastly improved. (Together wth the price hike.) Still best!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Sector is optimal for mobility drills. Good vantage points, but expect heavy crosswinds. There's hawker centre nearby. Good spot to replenish calories.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
One of the best gravel experience I had and the view is not bad! Will recommend visiting it during sunrise or sunset.

Also part of PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Large park with many local residents around that area walking and jogging. Seats are available around this park. There's playground for kids as well. There's also a canoe area in a separate building nearby and it's called "Passionwave". There's toilet facilities too. Quite a number of parking space available for those who came from far. Many companies and schools like to hold their events here as the space is spacious and it is convenient to come here for the bus stops are nearby too.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Well maintained park with walking paths around the reservoir. The sandy path by the water takes more effort than the shady paved path running along side. A good 5km round the park. Different gradients on the paved path add to a good workout. Encountered squirrels and a lone otter too.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Beautiful spot for a walk or jog! Bedok Reservoir's lush greenery and picturesque views of the water make it an ideal destination for outdoor enthusiasts. Well-maintained and clean facilities too. It’s always a nice 5 km walk !
